subject: Databases
title: GROUP BY Splits Data
description: A 60-second lesson on GROUP BY Splits Data.

hook.text: How many users per country? GROUP BY country turns one big table into groups, one per country.

buildup.text: SELECT country, COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY country. Each group gets its own aggregate.

discovery.text: You can group by multiple columns: GROUP BY country, city. Groups within groups.

twist.text: Every column in SELECT must either be in GROUP BY or wrapped in an aggregate function. Otherwise, error.

climax.text: GROUP BY is the key to reporting: revenue by month, signups by source, errors by type.

punchline.text: GROUP BY: split rows into buckets, then aggregate each.

quiz.question: Why must non-aggregated columns appear in GROUP BY?
options: The database needs to know which value to show when multiple rows are collapsed into one group
It is an optional best practice, not a requirement
GROUP BY only works with numeric columns
